summary: Gruber announces version 1.1 of bbdiff, a Perl script he wrote to invoke BBEdit's Find Differences command from the Mac OS X command line. The tool bridges the command line and BBEdit via AppleScript embedded in Perl. He notes a limitation where the script requires BBEdit's application name to be hard-coded, since AppleScript lacks a simple way to reference apps by creator code or bundle identifier. He also takes a mild jab at the convention of putting version numbers in application filenames.
key_points: bbdiff is a Perl script that communicates with BBEdit via AppleScript to enable command-line diffing
The tool bridges Mac OS X's Unix command line with BBEdit's native GUI capabilities
AppleScript's inability to reference applications by creator code or bundle identifier is a notable limitation
Gruber considers version numbers in application filenames to be outdated practice
money_quotes: While written in Perl, it communicates with BBEdit via AppleScript — it's a Perl script that runs an AppleScript.
The version-number-in-the-application-filename thing is a bit passe.
key_insight: An early example of Gruber's developer-toolsmith instincts, bridging Mac OS X's Unix underpinnings with its native application layer through creative scripting.
